title: senecio jacobaea subsp. erraticus
intro: Senecio jacobaea subsp. erraticus, also known as ragwort, is a biennial plant species belonging to the daisy family, characterized by its distinctive yellow flowers and bitter taste. Traditionally, the leaves and flowers of ragwort have been used in herbal medicine due to their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of ragwort include its potential to treat digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to stimulate the immune system. In herbal preparations, ragwort is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although it's essential to note that it should be used under the guidance of a qualified healthcare practitioner due to its potential toxicity.
